ocr.text: In the Q1 2024 financial report for Fresenius SE within the Healthcare Services & Medtech sector, the Hospital bed occupancy
rates and financial ratios reveal significant insights. The occupancy rates for the hospitals are as follows: Hospital A at 85.5%,
Hospital B at 78.2%, Hospital C at 90.1%, and Hospital D at 82.4%. These occupancy figures are crucial indicators of hospital
utilization and operational efficiency. Additionally, the financial ratios provide a comprehensive overview of the hospitals' fiscal
health. The price-to-earnings ratio (P/E Ratio) for Hospital A stands at 12.3, for Hospital B at 15.8, and for Hospital C at 11.5,
reflecting varying market valuations relative to earnings across the hospitals.
Furthermore, the current ratios, which measure liquidity, indicate that Hospital A has a current ratio of 1.5, Hospital B has a
current ratio of 1.3, and Hospital C has a current ratio of 1.6, while Hospital D's current ratio is not specified in the provided
data. These ratios suggest that all hospitals maintain adequate short-term financial health, with Hospital C demonstrating the
strongest liquidity position. Lastly, the debt-to-equity ratios highlight the financial leverage of these hospitals, with Hospital A
at 0.4, Hospital B at 0.5, and Hospital C at 0.3, indicating a relatively conservative use of debt across the board, which is
essential for long-term sustainability. This collective data underscores the operational performance and financial stability of
each hospital, crucial for stakeholders and decision-makers in the healthcare landscape.
